architecture.summary: Plugin-based sync: 3 providers (JIRA/GitHub/ADO) with core engine, circuit breaker, retry queue, hook lifecycle. GitHub most mature, JIRA/ADO missing locking and circuit breaker integration.

integrations.summary: JIRA REST API, GitHub REST API, ADO REST API. Real test instances: SWE2E JIRA project, anton-abyzov/specweave GitHub, EasyChamp/SpecWeaveSync ADO.

ui-ux.summary: N/A - CLI/backend sync feature, no UI. Error surfacing to CLI stdout/stderr is the only user-facing concern.

performance.summary: ADO 200 req/min PAT limit needs token bucket. JIRA uses 429 backoff. GitHub has pre-check rate limiting.

security.summary: PAT/API tokens already in config. No new credential handling needed. Audit logging for sync failures.

edge-cases.summary: 10 gaps identified: silent errors, race conditions (JIRA no locking), regex fragility (ADO), one-way sync (ADO), missing closure hooks, import creates docs not increments, inconsistent suffixes.
